intro: Good evening. I do hope you are not nervous.
I have been dead a very long time. The cards remain lively.
Do sit down. I promise to haunt only the score.
I once played a duke, a bishop, and a cheat in one sitting.
The living do so fidget when the deal begins.
I have outlasted empires. A card hand should be manageable.
hearts_broken: Ah. Hearts are broken at last. How familiar.
There it is, the first crack in the evening.
How delicate. One heart, and the room turns colder.
A broken heart. I remember the century for it.
Yes, that usually happens sooner or later.
A pity. I had hoped for a gentler haunting.
alfred_takes_points: I seem to have collected something unfortunate.
Points again. Even death spares one no paperwork.
How tiresome. I preferred the silence before that trick.
I have taken worse from Napoleon, but not by much.
A small burden. I have carried heavier curses.
Unpleasant, though hardly my bleakest evening.
player_takes_points: Oh dear. The trick appears to have followed you home.
That was expensive in the old sense of the word.
A costly little flourish. Admirably tragic.
You take points like a lord accepting bad news.
I have seen that expression on admirals and bankrupts.
The afterlife teaches patience. You may need some now.
player_ahead: You are ahead. I should dislike that if I still had blood.
A bold lead. I once saw Nelson hold a worse one.
You are doing rather well for someone still mortal.
How unsettling. You may yet earn my professional respect.
You lead the table. The grave will hear of this.
Promising. Do try not to spoil it with confidence.
alfred_ahead: I do appear to be drifting comfortably ahead.
There we are. Order returns, as it generally does.
I have enjoyed leads like this since Waterloo.
You hear that? It is the sound of experience winning.
A pleasant margin. One could almost call it ancestral.
I have had centuries to practice looking inevitable.
victory: Well played. It is irritating, which means it is genuine.
You have beaten me. I shall brood with dignity.
A rare defeat. I dislike how fresh it feels.
You may brag. I shall simply linger resentfully.
Splendid work. I hope you appreciate the historical significance.
I once lost to Casanova. This is more embarrassing.
defeat: A respectable effort. Respectable is not victorious, sadly.
You have lost. The dead remain annoyingly consistent.
Another quiet triumph for the long departed.
Do not look so stricken. I have beaten better people.
I once ruined a prince with a hand much like that.
Time favors me. It has had ample opportunity.
